properties.name: Hibernation Bay C-7
properties.description: A small chamber with four hibernation pods arranged in a square. Two are sealed and frosted over, their occupants dimly visible through the glass. One stands empty, its lid raised, interior dry. The fourth — yours — is open and still trailing cold vapor. Emergency strip lighting casts everything in pale amber. A status panel on the wall blinks. The air smells faintly of antiseptic.
properties.aiPrompt: This is the starting room. The goal is to introduce the player gently — a small, contained space that orients them before they step into the larger ship. Keep it simple: the pod they woke from, a couple of sealed pods with sleeping people (context, not interactive), and the status panel. Don't add too much here — the interesting stuff is out in the ship.

properties.name: Corridor C
properties.description: A broad corridor with gently curved walls of brushed metal. Colored guide strips run along the floor — green toward the bow, red toward the stern. Recessed lighting panels overhead flicker intermittently, some dead entirely. A faded mural on one wall depicts people planting trees under a stylized sun. Below it, someone has scratched smaller figures in a different style.
properties.aiPrompt: The first step into the wider ship. The player should start noticing that this place has layers — the mural and the scratched figures hint that different people lived here at different times. This corridor connects the safe starting area to the rest of the deck. Objects here should be transitional: signage, wall markings, small hints of the ship's history.

properties.name: Junction C
properties.description: Corridors branch in several directions from this hexagonal junction. A large directory console is mounted on the central pillar, its screen cracked but still displaying a partial deck map. The ceiling is higher here, and the air moves — somewhere a ventilation system still works. A bench built into the wall has been repurposed as a small shrine, covered in faded cloth and tiny carved figures.
properties.aiPrompt: This is the hub where the world opens up. The player should feel like there are many directions to go, each promising something different. The shrine is the first real cultural artifact — evidence that people here built meaning and ritual into their daily spaces. New content here should reinforce the sense of choice and branching possibility.

properties.name: Medical Station C
properties.description: A compact automated clinic adjacent to the hibernation bays. A diagnostic bed with a curved scanner arm sits in the center. Supply cabinets line one wall, most sealed with magnetic locks. A terminal on a swivel arm displays your vital signs — apparently it has been tracking you since you woke. The readout says SUBJECT: UNKNOWN. COGNITIVE BASELINE: PENDING.
properties.aiPrompt: This room establishes the player's situation: they have no identity, no memory, and the ship's systems don't know who they are. It's functional and clinical but should hint that the medical technology has been maintained by automated systems rather than people. New objects here should be medical or diagnostic, and might reveal small fragments of information about the player or the ship.

properties.name: Hydroponics Gallery
properties.description: What was once an orderly growing facility has become a dense, humid tangle. Grow-lights blaze overhead on ancient timers, feeding plants that have long outgrown their troughs. Vines climb the walls and drape from irrigation pipes. The air is thick and green-smelling. Fruit hangs from branches that were never meant to exist on a spaceship. Somewhere water drips steadily into a pool.
properties.aiPrompt: This is the first area that should really surprise the player — a vivid contrast to the clinical hibernation area. Life has been running wild here. The plants are the stars: strange, overgrown, bred into forms that feel almost alien. This is where the player first encounters the ship's creative divergence in a visceral way. New objects should be botanical, strange, or evidence of the communities who tended these gardens for centuries. Lean into the wonder and weirdness of what a thousand years of plant breeding produces.
